local-slm._comment: Local OpenAI-compatible endpoint (Ollama, vLLM, LM Studio). Point offload here to summarize tool output on a self-hosted small model at zero marginal cost. Ollama serves the OpenAI API at http://localhost:11434/v1/chat/completions, so set url to the host root. Providers may also set 'api_key_env' naming an env var with their own API key — required for plugin-initiated rerouting (env.route_request), where the caller's credential is never forwarded; leave it unset for local endpoints that need no auth.

gemini._comment: Public Gemini API (or Vertex AI) over the bare Gemini generateContent wire format — for tools you point at a base URL with an API key (Cline, Aider, Continue, Zed, opencode, …), exactly like the openai providers. No MITM needed: send requests to /provider/gemini/v1beta/models/<model>:streamGenerateContent. Set api_key_env if rerouting here; otherwise the caller's key/credential is forwarded.

antigravity._comment: Google Code Assist backend for the Antigravity CLI (agy), reached via the MITM ingress below (agy can't be pointed at a base URL). Same content model as 'gemini' but wrapped in the Code Assist envelope, hence format 'gemini-codeassist'. The caller's own Google OAuth bearer is forwarded upstream — Torana injects no auth. Two channels exist: prod (this) and 'daily' (dev); the mitm.hosts map routes each hostname to the matching provider.

mitm._comment: TLS-terminating CONNECT proxy for harnesses that can't be pointed at a base URL (the Antigravity CLI's stripped Go binary ignores endpoint env vars but honors HTTPS_PROXY and a custom CA via SSL_CERT_FILE). Disabled unless 'enabled' is true. Only chat calls (…:streamGenerateContent / …:generateContent) on the mapped hosts run through the provider pipeline; all other hosts and paths are tunneled/forwarded verbatim so login and telemetry keep working. Point agy at it with HTTPS_PROXY=http://<listen> SSL_CERT_FILE=<ca_dir>/bundle.pem. Keep 'listen' on localhost — it decrypts caller traffic — and NEVER commit ca_dir (the CA private key lives there) or add the CA to the system trust store.

plugins._comment: Order matters: schema_translator first (translate requests, reverse responses), then 'intent' (captures WHY each tool call is made into the shared cache), then ONE compactor. keyword_compactor (deterministic, local, free) and compactor (cheap-model offload, higher quality) are alternatives, not a pipeline — running both lets whichever is first starve the other. Both require 'intent' before them.

intent._comment: fill controls what the intent plugin does with a HISTORY tool call whose intent was never captured (the model organically omitted 'i'): 'heuristic' (default) fills it with a template derived from the call's arguments and the current task — required to keep multi-turn 'i' emission alive, since one 'i'-less call in history becomes a per-tool precedent the model imitates; 'off' leaves history untouched (only sensible for models that reliably honor required schema fields). Filled values are never cached and never drive compaction; watch torana_intent_filled_total to see how often a model needs propping up.

compactor._comment: max_offload_input_chars caps how much of a tool output is sent to the cheap summarizer. Omit or set 0 to send the COMPLETE tool output (default, unbounded). Set a positive value only if you want to bound offload cost/latency for very large outputs — it keeps the head+tail and drops the middle, which can discard relevant content.

pii._comment: PII detection. Scans tool results and BLOCKS the request (returns a provider-shaped error naming the PII type + line, never the value) if PII is found. 'provider' names a provider above used for the model-based scan — it MUST be a local/trusted endpoint because it receives raw tool output including any PII (e.g. 'local-slm'). A deterministic regex pre-filter (email, SSN, AWS key, private key) runs first and works even without a model. 'tools' is a tool-name allowlist (empty or ["*"] = all tool results). 'on_error' = "block" (fail-closed, default) or "allow" (fail-open). To enable, add "pii" to the order array above.

offload._comment: Cheap-model tool result summarization used by the compactor plugin. 'provider' must exist above and use the openai format (deepseek-v4-flash is only a default, not a hard dependency — any openai-compatible model works, including a local SLM via the 'local-slm' provider). 'api_key_env' names an env var holding a dedicated offload key. IMPORTANT: leave it empty ONLY when the offload provider shares the caller's auth; for cross-provider offload (e.g. a Claude- or OpenAI-key caller summarizing on DeepSeek) or a keyless local model, set a dedicated key here — otherwise the caller's credential is reused and will fail to authenticate against a different provider.

cache._comment: Cross-request plugin state (compacted tool results, PII verdicts, intents). 'backend' is "memory" (default, per-process, lost on restart) or "redis" (survives restarts, shared across proxy instances in a distributed deployment). 'ttl_seconds' defaults to 900. For redis, 'password_env' names an env var holding the password — never put the password in this file; 'prefix' namespaces keys so one Redis can serve several deployments.
